name: node-execa
title: node-execa
description:
better `child_process`
This is better in the following ways:
- Promise interface.
- Strips EOF from the output so you don't have to `stdout.trim()`.
- Supports shebang binaries cross-platform.
- Improved Windows support.
- Higher max buffer. 10 MB instead of 200 KB.
- Executes locally installed binaries by name.
- Cleans up spawned processes when the parent process dies.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-execa because better `child_process`.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-execa as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-execa with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-execa --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
